title: dianthus cretaceus subsp. cretaceus
intro: Dianthus cretaceus subsp. cretaceus, also known as the Cretan pink or Cretan carnation, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Dianthus cretaceus subsp. cretaceus can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems.
